compatibility.notes:
HNSW indexes are node-local on multi-node RAC. Works well up to
~50M vectors on single node (≤24 ECPUs). At 100M+ on multi-node
(≥64 ECPUs), requires hash-partitioned vector table with LOCAL
HNSW index for <50ms P95. See FF-202603-008.
Check if distributed HNSW is added in 26ai. Expected to remain node-local.
Same node-local limitation as ADB-S Serverless.
Same limitation. Dedicated infra doesn't change HNSW locality.
RAC with HNSW has same node-local constraint. Partitioning workaround applies.
AI Vector Search requires 23ai. Available in EE but HNSW performance limited without Exadata storage.

IVF (Inverted File) indexes ARE distributed across RAC nodes.
Works at 100M+ scale without partitioning. However, IVF has
lower recall than HNSW at same latency budget. Trade-off:
IVF for scale without redesign, HNSW+partitioning for best recall.
IVF distributed across RAC nodes. Exadata storage accelerates scans.
AI Vector Search requires 23ai. Available in EE but performance limited without Exadata.
